deadCode.description: The Windows Data Protection API (DPAPI) helper functions protectForCurrentUser and unprotectForCurrentUser are fully coded but never imported or used. Paired device secrets are written in plaintext inside trusted-devices.json.
deadCode.evidence: Grep search for 'protect' shows no references outside dpapi.ts itself.

deadCode.description: Stdout buffering logic exists in the child spawn block for new-conversation, but the server only returns on process close. There is no route or streaming socket that exposes real-time stdout chunks to the client.
Context menu items for chats and spaces (e.g., 'Pin chat', 'Rename chat', 'Delete local history', 'Rename Space') contain empty closures and perform no operational changes.
